candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary line, but it is not labelled as COGS/cost of sales related and appears as a standalone nature-of-expense item rather than a component of the selected COGS row 'Material and services'. To avoid unsupported allocation and double counting, do not add it to COGS.
Component of the standalone D&A block, not specifically tied to COGS; summary row exists, so component should not be chosen.
Potential D&A component, but no value shown and not COGS-specific.
Component of standalone D&A block, not specifically tied to COGS; summary row exists, so do not choose separately.
Specific depreciation component within the D&A block, not COGS-specific; summary row exists and this looks more like a non-core operating asset item.
Balancing row, not an economic expense line to map.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is only 'Material and services'. The statement is presented by nature, and the visible D&A rows are standalone operating lines rather than COGS-specific components. No explicit COGS/COR depreciation line is visible, so conservatively do not add any D&A separately to COGS.

candidates.why: Standalone operating D&A summary line above operating result, separate from the selected XSGA component rows. Because XSGA was built from specific operating-expense component rows that exclude this standalone D&A line, this summary row should be added separately. Prefer the summary over underlying components to avoid double counting.
Underlying component of the standalone D&A block. Do not choose because the summary row 'Depreciation and amortisation' exists.
Potential component of D&A, but no visible values and a higher-level summary row exists.
Underlying amortisation component within the D&A block. Do not choose because the summary row exists.
Specific depreciation component within the D&A block; not needed because the total D&A row is present.
Balancing row, not an economic expense line to map.

variables.notes: OK. The selected XSGA bucket consists of employee, product development, marketing, admin/consulting, and other operating expense components, and does not include the separate standalone row 'Depreciation and amortisation'. Since the statement is by nature and D&A is shown as its own operating line above EBIT, it should be added separately to XSGA rather than to COGS. Using the summary row avoids double counting component depreciation/amortisation lines.
